tossups.question: The protagonist of a historical novella by this author places a servant in a hole under the floor so that his head seems to be decapitated, and orders his wife to cut off that servant’s nose. Another novel by this author consists of two parallel diaries kept by a wife and her husband, who tries to spark an affair between her and their friend Kimura. This author of The (*) Key and The Secret History of the Lord of Musashi wrote a novel in which a 28-year-old engineer begins an affair with a 15-year-old waitress who reminds him of Mary Pickford. A cultural traditionalist invites his daughter and his son-in-law to a bunraku performance in a novel by this author that follows the waning marriage of Misako and Kaname. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of Naomi and Some Prefer Nettles.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Junichiro Tanizaki [accept names in either order]
tossups.question_sanitized: The protagonist of a historical novella by this author places a servant in a hole under the floor so that his head seems to be decapitated, and orders his wife to cut off that servant's nose. Another novel by this author consists of two parallel diaries kept by a wife and her husband, who tries to spark an affair between her and their friend Kimura. This author of The (*) Key and The Secret History of the Lord of Musashi wrote a novel in which a 28-year-old engineer begins an affair with a 15-year-old waitress who reminds him of Mary Pickford. A cultural traditionalist invites his daughter and his son-in-law to a bunraku performance in a novel by this author that follows the waning marriage of Misako and Kaname. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of Naomi and Some Prefer Nettles.

tossups.question: One story by this author includes a synopsis of the film Les Diaboliques, which the narrator remembers while in a drug-addled delusion. The protagonist of another work by this author idolizes Mary Pickford as well as his dance instructor, Madam Shlemskaya. In one work, this author comments on the use of large eaves and describes the (*) toilet as a place of “spiritual repose.” Joji pays for English lessons for his eventual dominatrix in one novel by this man, who wrote about how a disdain for “Osaka traits” is shared by the unhappily- married Kaname and Misako. In another novel, Taeko falls in love with Itakura to the disapproval of Sachiko, one of the title siblings. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of Some Prefer Nettles and The Makioka Sisters.

Joji pays for English lessons for his eventual dominatrix in one novel by this man, who wrote about how a disdain for "Osaka traits" is shared by the unhappily- married Kaname and Misako. In another novel, Taeko falls in love with Itakura to the disapproval of Sachiko, one of the title siblings. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of Some Prefer Nettles and The Makioka Sisters.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about depictions of the Japanese custom of teeth blackening in literature, for 10 points each.
parts: Taira no Atsumori’s youth is emphasized by his blackened teeth in a popular story from the Tale of Heike that was adapted into many plays in this classic Japanese form, in which actors wear stylized masks.

The title eccentric neglects to blacken her teeth in a Heian-era story titled for the “Lady who Loved [these things].” In Kobo Abe’s The Woman in the Dunes, the protagonist is collecting these things when he becomes trapped with the title character.
Misako thinks of O-Hisa’s archaic habit of teeth-blackening as “filthy and barbarous” in this Japanese author’s novel Some Prefer Nettles.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about depictions of the Japanese custom of teeth blackening in literature, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: Taira no Atsumori's youth is emphasized by his blackened teeth in a popular story from the Tale of Heike that was adapted into many plays in this classic Japanese form, in which actors wear stylized masks.

The title eccentric neglects to blacken her teeth in a Heian-era story titled for the "Lady who Loved [these things]." In Kobo Abe's The Woman in the Dunes, the protagonist is collecting these things when he becomes trapped with the title character.
Misako thinks of O-Hisa's archaic habit of teeth-blackening as "filthy and barbarous" in this Japanese author's novel Some Prefer Nettles.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these things about Chikamatsu's plays:
parts: A Chikamatsu play partially titled after this character includes a scene where either white or red dye will be dumped from the cistern of the Castle of Lions to indicate the result of a parlay. In real life, this man conquered Dutch Taiwan and founded the Kingdom of Tungming.

The central character of this later novel by a different author realizes that his idea of the Eternal Woman is embodied by the doll of Koharu in a Bunraku production of The Love Suicides at Sonezaki .
answers_sanitized: Coxinga [or The Battles of Coxinga ; or Watonai]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, name these things about Chikamatsu's plays:
parts_sanitized: A Chikamatsu play partially titled after this character includes a scene where either white or red dye will be dumped from the cistern of the Castle of Lions to indicate the result of a parlay. In real life, this man conquered Dutch Taiwan and founded the Kingdom of Tungming.

The central character of this later novel by a different author realizes that his idea of the Eternal Woman is embodied by the doll of Koharu in a Bunraku production of The Love Suicides at Sonezaki .

bonuses.leadin: In one novel by this author, a man named Sonomura cracks a code based on Poe’s The Gold-Bug to figure out where a murder is going to happen, which he then subsequently observes by using peepholes. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of the recently-translated The Devils in Daylight. Another book by him translated into English in 2016 is The Maids.
Tanizaki’s The Maids retells this novel from the servants’ point of view. This Tanizaki novel is named for a Osaka family whose fortunes have declined.
In 2004, Howard Hibbett translated this novel by Tanizaki into English, which takes the form of alternating diary entries made by Ikiko and her unnamed husband.
answers_sanitized: The Makioka Sisters [or Sasameyuki or Light Snow]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one novel by this author, a man named Sonomura cracks a code based on Poe's The Gold-Bug to figure out where a murder is going to happen, which he then subsequently observes by using peepholes. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this author of the recently-translated The Devils in Daylight. Another book by him translated into English in 2016 is The Maids.
Tanizaki's The Maids retells this novel from the servants' point of view. This Tanizaki novel is named for a Osaka family whose fortunes have declined.
In 2004, Howard Hibbett translated this novel by Tanizaki into English, which takes the form of alternating diary entries made by Ikiko and her unnamed husband.

bonuses.leadin: This character, who skims an unexpurgated edition of The Arabian Nights for the lewd passages, is fascinated with the black teeth of his father-in-law’s mistress. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who spends the later chapters of the novel in which he appears sleeping with a half-Korean, half-Russian prostitute named Louise.
Kaname is the protagonist of Some Prefer Nettles, a novel by this Japanese author of The Makioka Sisters.

Kaname sees traditional bunraku plays at Awaji Island and in this nearby city, which is where bunraku was invented. Chikamatsu Monzaemon moved from Kyoto to this city in the Kansai region to write plays.
answers_sanitized: Junichiro Tanizaki [accept names in reverse order]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This character, who skims an unexpurgated edition of The Arabian Nights for the lewd passages, is fascinated with the black teeth of his father-in-law's mistress. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this character who spends the later chapters of the novel in which he appears sleeping with a half-Korean, half-Russian prostitute named Louise.
Kaname is the protagonist of Some Prefer Nettles, a novel by this Japanese author of The Makioka Sisters.

Kaname sees traditional bunraku plays at Awaji Island and in this nearby city, which is where bunraku was invented. Chikamatsu Monzaemon moved from Kyoto to this city in the Kansai region to write plays.

bonuses.leadin: One of the title characters,Yukiko, resists getting married; another title character, Taeko, has a scandalous fling with Itakura, a man of a much lower class. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel about a proud Osaka family whose wealth has recently declined.
This author of The Makioka Sisters wrote a novel about the love affair between Sonoko Kakiuchi and the art student Mitsuko in Quicksand.
Tanizaki also wrote this essay on Japanese aesthetics which contrasts “light” and “dark” elements. It contains a passage discussing the beauty of toilets.
answers_sanitized: In Praise of Shadows [or In'ei Raisan]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: One of the title characters,Yukiko, resists getting married; another title character, Taeko, has a scandalous fling with Itakura, a man of a much lower class. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel about a proud Osaka family whose wealth has recently declined.
This author of The Makioka Sisters wrote a novel about the love affair between Sonoko Kakiuchi and the art student Mitsuko in Quicksand.
Tanizaki also wrote this essay on Japanese aesthetics which contrasts "light" and "dark" elements. It contains a passage discussing the beauty of toilets.

bonuses.leadin: In one of his works, Joji trains his wife to be a Western woman, but she ends up controlling him. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Japanese author of Naomi, who wrote of Kaname’s struggles with East versus West in Some Prefer Nettles.
Tanizaki’s last published work was this novel, in which Utsugi becomes attracted to his daughter-in-law, Satsuko.
Diary of a Mad Old Man is not to be confused with this earlier author’s story “Diary of a Madman”. This Ukranian author wrote of Chichikov’s efforts to buy deceased serfs in Dead Souls.
answers: Diary of a Mad Old Man [or Futen Rojin Nikki]

packet.name: Bellevue + Michigan A + Georgia Tech FINAL

answers_sanitized: Diary of a Mad Old Man [or Futen Rojin Nikki]
